english: the harvest had been bad; in a special sense in medicine: the three humours of the body, air (v. rluń (rlung)), bile, and phlegm, gen. called त्रिदोष ynod-byéd nyés-pa ysum (gnod byed nyes pa gsum) the three noxious matters (most diseases being ascribed to a derangement of one of them). — 2. moral fault, offence, sin, crime, nyés-pai skyon (nyes pa'i skyon), being contaminated by a crime Dzl.; lus dań ńág-gi (lus dang ngag gi) (or ńai (nga'i)) nyés-pa (nyes pa) sin in word and deed Dzl.; nyés-pa byéd-pa (nyes pa byed pa) to commit a fault, a crime; to sin, frq.; also: mi źig-la nyés-pa byuń (mi zhig la nyes pa byung) a slip has occurred to a person Dzl.; bdág-la nyés-pa ći źig yód-de ma ynań (bdag la nyes pa ci zhig yod de ma gnang) what crime have I committed, that you will not give me permission? Dzl. — 3. punishment C. *nyé-miy* (*nye mig*) id., resp. *ka-nyé* (*bka' nyes*); nye-pa póg-kán* (nyes pa phog mkhan) he that has got a punishment. II. vb. to commit an offence, odis ći nyés-te bzuń ('dis ci nyes te bzung) what offence has he committed that he is taken prisoner? Dzl. (cf. above); sńón-čad bdág-gis nyés-pa bden (sngon chad bdag gis nyes pa bden) it is true that formerly I committed a fault Dzl.; sńár ma sbrán-pa nyés-so (sngar ma sbran pa nyes so) the not reporting sooner was a fault Dzl.; yyógs-pa nyés-so (g.yogs pa nyes so) you have committed a fault by covering... Dzl.; bdag nyés-na (bdag nyes na) if harm is done to me; hence ći nyés (ci nyes) in a general sense: kyod ći nyés-pa smros śig (khyod ci nyes pa smros shig) tell me what has happened to you Dzl.; btsón-na ći nyés (btson na ci nyes) quid mali, si vendideris? Dzl.; mi drán-nam ći nyes (mi dran nam ci nyes) is she out of her senses, or, what is the matter with her? Dzl.; ći nyés-na (ci nyes na) why, ći nyés-na kán-pai náń-na rdziń-bu bskyil (ci nyes na khang pa'i nang na rdzing bu bskyil) why is there a pond within the house? Dzl.; ma nyés-pai yró-ba (ma nyes pa'i 'gro ba) innocent beings Mil.; ma nyés-pa phyir byuń (ma nyes pa phyir byung) he came out again unhurt Dzl.; nyés-byas (nyes byas) a wicked action, a sin Cs.; nyés-ltuń (nyes ltung) sin, sinful deed, trespass, nyés-ltuń-gis phog (nyes ltung gis phog) he has been overtaken by a sin Mil.

pf. and imp. nyos (nyos), 1. to buy, dńul brgyas (dngul brgyas) for a hundred rupees; nyó-(ba-)po (nyo ba po) a buyer, purchaser, nyo-(ba-)mo (nyo ba mo) fem.; nyó-mkhan (nyo mkhan) a buyer, customer; nyó-to (nyo tho) account, bill; nyo-tshoń (nyo tshong) commerce, traffic; nyo-tshoń byéd-pa (nyo tshong byed pa) to trade. — 2. to take at rent, to take the lease (of a field, by buying the crop).
soiled, dirtied, made unclean, e.g. of victuals Mil.; nyóg-ma (nyog ma) Sch., čhu-nyóg (chu nyog) Lex. muddy, foul water; nyog-nyóg-po (nyog nyog po) confused (story) Tar.
Sch.: too soft; nyog-nyóń (nyog nyong) Sch. soft, tender, weak, inclined to weep; *śés-nyog-čan* (*shes nyog can*) (for yćés-nyog-čan (gces nyog can)) dandling, fondling W.

(seldom without -pa (-pa)), Ssk. क्लेश 1. misery, trouble, pain, frq.; also used as a verb: nyon-móńs-śiń (nyon mongs shing); tśá-bas nyon-móńs-te (tsha bas nyon mongs te) molested by the heat Dzl.; nyon-móńs-par gyúr-ba (nyon mongs par gyur ba) to get into trouble Dzl.; nyon ma móńs-sam (nyon ma mongs sam) had you to experience any hardship? Dzl. — 2. in a restricted sense: the misery of sin, nyon-móńs-pa-las phan-pai don med (nyon mongs pa las phan pa'i don med) this does not avail for being delivered from such misery Dzl.; sin, nyon-móńs-pai nad (nyon mongs pa'i nad), dri-ma (dri ma) Dzl.; sér-sna-la sógs-pai nyon-móńs-pa (ser sna la sogs pa'i nyon mongs pa) avarice and other sins S.O.; nyon-móńs-pa-méd-pa (nyon mongs pa med pa) free from sin, sinless S.O.; nyon-móńs-čan-gyis nyá-śa nyos (nyon mongs can gyis nya sha nyos) Zam. the offender buys the flesh of a fish.
1. v. nyó-ba (nyo ba). 2. a rectangle Cs.
imp. of nyál-ba (nyal ba); nyól-ba (nyol ba) prov. for nyál-ba (nyal ba).
imp. of nyó-ba (nyo ba); nyos-mi (nyos mi) a slave Cs.

1 a pestilential disease, epidemic, or contagious disorder, plague, mdze dań brum-bu ynyan (mdze dang brum bu gnyan) Ma.; ynyan-nád (gnyan nad) id.; ynyan-dúg (gnyan dug) a poison against, or a remedy for the plague Med.; dka-ynyán (dka' gnyan) a destructive plague Sch. — 2. a species of wild sheep, argali (Ovis ammon).
